passage.title: Walking Plan Check
passage.text: Our field trip team tested two ways to walk through the museum last month. Hana used one detailed hallway map, but Evan used short turn cards. After a trip rehearsal, Evan's group moved between rooms more calmly because the turn cards helped them focus on the next step. The teachers timed each route and marked the stops on a chart. When Hana saw the difference, she changed to short turn cards. Later, her group followed the museum path with less confusion. The students talked about the test before choosing a better method. The group learned that simple path reminders can help groups move smoothly.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mostly about?
choices: Finding a clearer way to move through the museum
questions.rationale: The whole passage follows one test and what the group learned from it.

questions.prompt: What did Evan's group use to walk through the museum?
questions.rationale: The second sentence directly tells which tool or place was used.

questions.prompt: What did the group check or write down?
questions.rationale: The fourth sentence explains what the group checked or recorded.

questions.prompt: What does difference mean in the fifth sentence?
choices: A loud sound in a room
The way two things are not the same
questions.rationale: Difference means the two results did not match each other.

questions.prompt: What does route mean in the first sentence?
choices: The way from one place to another
A person who visits a club
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from how it is used in the passage.

questions.prompt: Why did Hana switch to short turn cards?
choices: The teacher told the student to stop working
The group wanted to decorate the room more
The first tool was missing from the class
She thought clear turn cards would help her group stay on course
questions.rationale: The student changed after seeing which method worked better.
